top_stories.title: SailPoint to Acquire Entro in Reported $200 Million Deal
top_stories.summary: SailPoint has announced plans to acquire Israel-based Entro Security in a reported $200 million deal. Entro specializes in non-human identity (NHI) and secrets security, and the acquisition will expand SailPoint's identity governance capabilities into machine and service account credentials.

top_stories.title: What is new in Microsoft Entra: June 2026 updates
top_stories.summary: Microsoft Entra's June 2026 update introduces phishing-resistant MFA for Linux desktops, expanded passkey support on Windows, and changes to Self-Service Password Reset identity verification. The release also includes new Conditional Access enforcement updates.

top_stories.title: IBM and Microsoft partner to automate identity threat response
top_stories.summary: IBM and Microsoft have launched a collaborative service that integrates Microsoft's security telemetry with IBM's managed response capabilities to automate identity threat detection and remediation. The partnership is designed to close the gap between identifying identity-based threats and executing governed responses.

top_stories.title: Microsoft enhances Entra and Defender to counter AI-driven identity attacks
top_stories.summary: Microsoft has enhanced its Entra and Defender products to counter AI-driven identity attacks. The updates address AI-automated reconnaissance, personalized social engineering, and vulnerability chaining targeting identity systems.

top_stories.title: Novo Nordisk Breach Exposes Software Development Pipeline Risk
top_stories.summary: Novo Nordisk suffered a breach involving a leaked GitHub token that exposed vulnerabilities in their software development pipeline. The incident highlights failures in secrets management as an identity and access management problem, specifically around how credentials and tokens are governed within development environments.

news.title: Hundreds of AI-powered iOS apps found exposing credentials
news.summary: Researchers from Wake Forest University analyzed 444 iOS applications with LLM features and found 282 exposing exploitable credentials or backend access mechanisms. The study identified credential leakage via network traffic interception across 13 app categories, highlighting insecure handling of API keys and backend access tokens in mobile AI applications.

news.title: Show HN: Lelu – authorization engine that catches manipulated AI agents
news.summary: An open-source authorization engine called Lelu has been released on GitHub, targeting AI agent access control. The project features confidence-aware gating, human-in-the-loop review, policy-as-code, and audit trail capabilities for controlling manipulated or compromised AI agents.

news.title: Klue OAuth breach victim list grows as Icarus hackers claim attack
news.summary: Klue confirmed a security breach in which threat actors stole OAuth tokens used to connect to customers' Salesforce environments. The attack was claimed by a new extortion group called "Icarus," with the victim list continuing to grow.

news.title: Every AI Agent Is an Identity. Most Organizations Don't Treat Them That Way
news.summary: Token Security published an analysis arguing that AI agents represent a new class of identity requiring governance, as they can access data, trigger workflows, deploy code, and interact with critical business systems with minimal oversight. The piece frames AI agents as an emerging identity and access management challenge, calling for organizations to apply identity governance principles to non-human identities.

news.title: Webinar: How attackers bypass MFA and how defenders can respond
news.summary: A webinar addresses how modern phishing techniques, including Device Code phishing, can bypass MFA protections to gain unauthorized access to corporate accounts. The session covers behavioral AI-based detection methods and automated response workflows as defensive countermeasures.

news.title: Klue breach lead to Salesforce data theft, Huntress affected
news.summary: A breach at Klue, a market intelligence platform, began with a compromised integration credential and cascaded into theft of customer data across connected platforms including Salesforce, affecting cybersecurity vendor Huntress among others. The incident illustrates how a single compromised credential in a third-party integration can trigger a chain of downstream data breaches across multiple organizations.

news.title: Forget Data Leakage: Shadow AI's Real Threat Is Access Control
news.summary: The article argues that shadow AI has evolved from a data leakage problem into an access control problem, where unsanctioned AI tools and agents operating with user-level permissions pose authorization and identity governance risks. The piece frames the core threat around AI systems inheriting or abusing access rights, which falls directly within IAM and access management concerns.

news.title: Salesforce Disables Klue App Integration After OAuth Token Abuse Exposes Customer Data
news.summary: Salesforce disabled the Klue Battlecards app integration on its platform following a security incident on June 11, 2026, in which OAuth token abuse exposed customer data. The incident involves the misuse of OAuth tokens to gain unauthorized access, prompting Salesforce to block the integration until further notice.

news.title: FIFA Bug Exposes World Cup Streams to Remote Takeover
news.summary: A security vulnerability in FIFA's systems exposed World Cup streams to potential remote takeover due to unenforced Microsoft Entra access controls. The flaw could have allowed an attacker to hijack broadcast streams, highlighting a failure in proper Entra access control enforcement.

news.title: Orphaned AI Agents: How to Find Hidden Access Risks Inside Your Network
news.summary: The article addresses the identity governance challenge posed by orphaned AI agents that retain standing privileges after their creators leave an organization. It focuses on the lack of accountability and visibility into who authorized AI agent access to enterprise systems, framing it as an access management and identity governance problem.

news.title: Zero-Touch OAuth for MCP
news.summary: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) has released a stable Enterprise-Managed Authorization extension that enables centralized provisioning of MCP server access through existing identity providers. The extension eliminates per-app OAuth flows by allowing organizations to pre-provision user access to MCP servers, delivering connected server access automatically on first login.

news.title: Google Toldl Researchers 'Nice Catch!' Then Denied Bug Bounty For Flaw It Still Hasn't Fixed
news.summary: Security researcher Justin O'Leary discovered a privilege-escalation vulnerability in Google's Config Connector that Google initially classified as high-priority and high-severity. Google subsequently denied the bug bounty by reclassifying the behavior as "working as intended" while leaving the flaw unpatched.

news.title: Microsoft Entra ID single sign-on for the Starburst connector in Power BI (Preview)
news.summary: Microsoft has released a preview feature enabling Entra ID single sign-on for the Starburst connector in Power BI. When enabled, users querying semantic models in DirectQuery mode authenticate to Starburst using their own Entra ID identity, subject to Starburst's row-level and column-level access policies.

news.title: Semperis Appoints Jimmy McNary as Deputy Federal CTO to Drive Identity Security Strategy Across the Public Sector
news.summary: Semperis has appointed Jimmy McNary as Deputy Federal CTO to lead identity security strategy for its public sector division. McNary is a cybersecurity veteran with 25+ years of experience who previously served as VP of Federal Solutions.

news.title: Sweeping Credential-Harvesting Heist Compromises 30K+ Fortinet Devices
news.summary: A large-scale credential-harvesting attack has compromised over 30,000 Fortinet devices across nearly 200 countries. Attackers have compiled a list of working credentials from the compromised devices, actively targeting organizations across various sectors.

news.title: The Top 10 Attack Surface Exposures in 2026
news.summary: The article discusses top attack surface exposures predicted for 2026, including credential theft and session token exposure via vulnerabilities like "MongoBleed." It touches on IAM-adjacent topics such as credential reuse, brute-forced admin panels, and session token theft, but appears to be a broader security overview rather than a focused IAM piece.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant bot to bypass account security and reset passwords, enabling Instagram account takeovers. Instructions for the attack circulated on Telegram, with confirmed victims including the Obama White House and U.S. Space Force accounts being defaced with pro-Iranian content.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, triggering a congressional inquiry from both chambers. CISA is still working to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor exposed highly privileged AWS GovCloud account credentials and internal system details in a public GitHub repository until this past weekend. The leaked repository contained files describing CISA's internal software build, test, and deployment processes alongside the privileged access credentials.
